title: Resinous Slovakian Wheat Flatbread
description: A chewy resinous flatbread inspired by Slovakian tradition, enriched with fast-acting yeast for a quick double rise and balanced for post-workout nutrition.
instructions: In a large bowl combine 250g wheat flour and 5g instant dry yeast, mixing evenly.
Add 150ml warm water, 5g salt, 10g honey, and 15ml olive oil to the dry mix, stirring until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 8 minutes until elastic and slightly tacky.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise for 30 minutes at room temperature until doubled in size.
Punch down the dough, divide it into 4 equal portions, shaping each into a flat oval about 1cm thick.
Preheat a talavera plate in a dry skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes to reach a steady temperature.
Place one flatbread on the hot plate, covering it with a lid to trap steam, and cook for 4 minutes until bubbles form and edges lift.
Flip the flatbread, add a drizzle of olive oil, then cover again and cook for another 3 minutes until golden and chewy.
Repeat steps 7-8 for the remaining portions, keeping the heated plate ready.
Let the finished flatbreads rest for 5 minutes before serving, optionally sprinkling with a pinch of sea salt.
